title: The Solar Expedition of Stars and Snories
summary: A mole pilot, an eager explorer, and a stowaway mouse embark on a daring journey to the sun from a suburban backyard. Traveling in a tiny, high-tech spaceship equipped with heat shields and oxygen, the crew navigates past the cratered moon and explores the mechanics of gravity. They witness the blazing spectacle of sunspots and solar flares while discussing the biology of deep-sea creatures and the nature of distant stars. After a successful observation of the solar corona, they perform a stellar U-turn to safely return home to Earth.

highlightQuote.text: But in order to find some science questions, we need to do this: keep telling the story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay.
Stars and snories.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Snories is it?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Uncle Griscko.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, hi Sidseeker, nice to see you again. What are you up to today, Sidseeker?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Stars and snories. There's Uncle Griscko.
Oh, what's stars and snories, Sidseeker?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Stars and snories is when you put up some stars and you tell a story.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, that sounds amazing, Sidseeker. Now, Uncle Griscko, did you have any science questions that you wanted to talk about with me?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: No. But in order to find some science questions, we need to do this: keep telling the story and then you'll find out.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I see, Sidseeker. Now you mentioned stars as part of stars and snories. What do you want to talk about about stars? Because the stars in stars and snories are just pretend, of course. They're just the light that we make on the ceiling in little dots using the flashlight shining through the basket. But real stars, now that's a different story. Do you want to talk about real stars?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, pretend ones.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, pretend stars. Okay. Well, Sidseeker, maybe you and I can teleport to a place that's very close to the sun. The sun is a star. It's the closest one. It's the star that our planet Earth orbits around, round and round. And the light and heat from the sun are what give us all the light and heat we have on Earth. Well, most of it anyway. All the daylight and all the heat from the sun that makes the weather and the seasons. It all comes from the sun.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm Sidseeker and I'd like to play my own pretend space mission.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Alexander, Sidseeker, would you like to come with us on our space mission? Me and Uncle Griscko can go to the sun.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm going to the pretend stars up in this room. They're very busy room.
Oh, the pretend stars on the ceiling. Sidseeker said well, maybe we should do that mission instead. Do you want to do that, Uncle Griscko?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Isabella's bringing me up with her arms.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Sidseeker, but it looks like that might not get Alexander high enough to really reach those pretend stars.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, she can just look at them.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That's true. But Sidseeker, we could take Uncle Griscko's spaceship and fly all the way up to the ceiling.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, what about flying up to the sun?
Well, we could do that too.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: I want to do the sun.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, okay. And so Uncle Griscko said, come on over to my house, Sidseeker. I have in my backyard a little tiny spaceship. And it could fit me and you and maybe one cat, if you know any cats that want to come along. Do you know any?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Nope. No cats want to come along. What about you, wonderful Alexander?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, I have a very big chore to do sometimes. It's called 'Go to my story' (store). Isabella's lunchbox. Tamagotchi store. So I can't go.
Oh yeah, a big responsibility. So yeah, you can't come with us on our space mission, Alexander. You better stay home and do your work. And so Sidseeker and Uncle Griscko got in the tiny spaceship.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: And nobody else.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: And nobody else. Okay.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: But Victor jumped in with them.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: And Sidseeker said, oh my gosh, who's this little mouse?
It's Victor. He just jumped right in because he wanted to explore the sun too.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Wow. And Uncle Griscko said, well, the three of us can just barely fit, especially because I'm a mole, so I'm pretty small. And you're a mouse, so you're even smaller. And Sidseeker is bigger than us, but we all fit in this little ship because there are three seats that are just our size and we can buckle in for safety. And this ship has a very good way to protect us from the heat of the sun so we can fly very close. But there's another important thing: the sun is very bright, so we're going to have to wear special sunglasses, okay? Remember to put them on when it's time. And we'll also darken our windows so it doesn't get too bright.
Yeah, and how does it protect us?
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: I know Victor. It's a very exciting mission to go to the sun and you want to understand the safety features of this ship, said Uncle Griscko. Well, it has heat shielding that keeps the heat from the sun away from us, and air conditioning to keep us cool also, and the windows darken so it doesn't get too bright, and also, just in case, we all have sunglasses to protect our eyes. What other questions do you have?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: But sometimes the knees peek out from their blankets.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Sometimes the knees peek out, that's true. And so Uncle Griscko said, I'm just going to check over everything on the ship. Check, check, check. Make sure the navigation is working, and the engines are working, and the air conditioning is working, and the windows are working, and the steering is working, and the seats are working, and the seatbelts are working. Everything's working. Okay, everybody, buckle up and we'll start the countdown.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Ten, nine, eight, seven, six...
Five, four, three, two, one, blast off!
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Actually, we forgot to do zero. Zero! Blast off!

And so Victor and Uncle Griscko and Sidseeker were pushed down into their seats as the rocket ship pushed them straight up into the air. And they lifted up from Uncle Griscko's backyard and very quickly they could see his house getting smaller and smaller as they went high up in the air. And pretty soon they could see the whole forest shrinking to a tiny green dot, and all of the Earth became a round ball.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Sometimes rockets are shaking.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh yes, they were shaking, shake, shake, shake, shake, shake as they made their way up through the atmosphere. And then suddenly, all was still and the ride on the rocket ship became very smooth because they were out of the atmosphere. The atmosphere around Earth is the air that we breathe, and all of the clouds and wind and rain and everything. It's in a thin layer all around the Earth. And when you go up into space, you go out of the atmosphere and there's no air anymore. And so they were out in the blackness of space.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Did you forget your oxygen?
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh no, said Uncle Griscko, there's plenty of oxygen inside this ship for us to breathe, nothing to worry about.
But what about if knees peek out? That's okay, said Victor, that's okay. Because knees are only on Earth, not there. That's true. Yeah, and they're only real, but you're pretend.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: That's true. And so Uncle Griscko steered the ship, firing the engines to adjust their course so they would go in the right direction.
And then they stopped on the moon.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Well, first they had to get there, and it's a long way to get to the sun. The sun is very big and very far away. And so first, they passed by the moon so they could have a look. Sidseeker looked out the window and said, 'Wow, I've never seen it so close before.' And Uncle Griscko said, 'Yeah, isn't it amazing? You can see so much detail, all the big beautiful craters and moon rocks.' And Victor said, 'I want to see, I want to see!' And Sidseeker said, 'Oh, do you need a boost to get up to the window?' And Victor said, 'Yeah, I'm a little small.' And so Sidseeker held him up so he could see out the window too. And Victor said, 'Wow, I must be the first mouse that's seen the moon up close like that. Pretty amazing.' And then he let go and he floated up in the air and Sidseeker said, 'Whoa, we've got to be careful!'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I caught him! I got... I got a net! Here, Sidseeker, you borrow the net. You try to scoop... you try to scoop Victor.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Sidseeker said thank you and he scooped Victor out of the air and pulled him back down to his seat safely. And he said, Victor, it's probably a good idea to stay buckled in while we're on the ship because we're weightless when we're in space and so we'll just float around inside our ship. And Victor said, 'Wow, I didn't know we'd be weightless in outer space.' Sidseeker says yes, Uncle Griscko can tell us all about it.
Uncle Griscko, why are we weightless in space?
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Well, it's because of a thing called gravity. Do you know about gravity?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: No, I don't. It's just like gravity goldfish. Gravity goldfish is a fish. He's only pretend. He's one of Uncle's friends. And only in a pond. He's only on the floor. He lives with Mini Mango. He's only pretend. He's only in songs.
I see. And what kind of things can gravity goldfish do?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Just swim, and water towers, and gravity, and sometimes just swim.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Now, what does he do with gravity?
I don't know. Sometimes he likes to make water towers. I know that from him.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: I see. Well, gravity is what when we're on Earth pulls us down to the ground. Like if you're holding a toy and then you let go, what happens? It falls down. And gravity is pulling it down to the ground all the time. Otherwise, we'd float away. And when you jump in the air, you're pushing your body against gravity and you go up, but then the gravity just keeps pulling you down all the time. So then you fall back down once you slow down. You jump up in the air and then fall back down. And that's why a rocket ship, to get off of the Earth, needs a really powerful rocket to push it straight up in the air. But then, you know what happens when we're far away from the Earth?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: What?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Then we don't feel its gravity as much. The gravity is weaker. So then we can just float around. It doesn't pull us back down anymore. And so we just float through the air. That's like what just happened to Victor. He floated up out of his seat. So we had to put him back down and buckle him in for safety.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Here, I'll buckle him. Or Slickdorito, you buckle him. Slickdorito buckled him tightly and then he went back home in his rocket. His tiny rocket. But he remembered he had to go to the moon with Slickdorito. So they zoomed, and they zoomed, and they zoomed, and they landed on the moon.
They did? In their tiny mouse rocket? Wow. What did they do on the moon?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: They collected space rocks and then went back home and then ate them and then took a nap.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what did they do with the space rocks?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They made rock mice.
Oh wow, space rock mice. That sounds cool.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: So they can go on pretend space missions.

I see. And so Sidseeker and Uncle Griscko and Victor passed by the moon in their ship and then Uncle Griscko turned on the rocket engine again to accelerate them towards the sun. Accelerate means when you go faster and faster. And so the rocket fired and they went faster and faster.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: And hey, put on the sunglasses!
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, good idea, said Uncle Griscko. Yeah, we'll put on the air conditioning, we'll put on... breathe some oxygen. Yeah, we'll do all those things. And so to keep themselves safe, they put on the air conditioning and their sunglasses and they darkened the windows and they made sure the heat shields were working. And then they started to get closer to the sun.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And did you have your sunglasses? And did you drink a sip of your oxygen?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Iz, did you know oxygen is a gas? That means it's not a liquid like water. It's more like the air. In fact, oxygen is part of the air that we breathe all the time. The air that's all around us. And so the oxygen on the ship is for them to breathe.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, but Dad, I don't know what I'd like to do with them. I don't know what they are, but sometimes I like to talk to Uncle Griscko.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, do you have any questions, said Uncle Griscko?
Uncle Griscko, what teeth do anglerfish have and do their lures shine?
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, now that is a very good question. Anglerfish are a kind of fish called a predator. Do you know what that means? A predator is a fish that hunts and eats other fish. And so it has to bite them to eat them, and so it has teeth that are sharp so it can bite. And is their lure bright? The lure is bright. The anglerfish lives in the dark ocean and its lure is a little glowing thing that floats in front of its face. And the reason it has that is because some tiny little fish and shrimp are interested. They're curious about it. They think it might be food. They don't know that the anglerfish is waiting there to eat them. So they swim right up to the anglerfish's mouth, not knowing that the anglerfish is even there because they're just trying to look at the lure, and then the anglerfish opens its mouth and chomp! Eats them up. Do you have any other questions for Uncle Griscko?
Do jellyfish have teeth or lures?
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Now, that is a good question too. Jellyfish are a very different kind of animal. They don't have an eye to eyes to see or brains to think with. They don't have arms and legs.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Or...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They just have a big soft, squishy body.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Do they have teeth?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No, they don't have teeth either.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Or lures?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No, but they do have long tentacles. The tentacles are long, stringy things that are kind of like legs, but they don't use them to walk. But some jellyfish use them to catch little tiny fish and shrimp. And they grab them with their tentacles and then they pull their tentacles up to their body and eat them, but they don't eat them by biting.
Uncle Griscko, what are shrimp?
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Shrimp are little swimming creatures that look kind of like bugs. They have lots of little legs and a hard shell and antennae.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Just like butterflies.
Yeah, but they don't have wings, they swim in the water.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: And what do starfish do?
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Good question. Starfish don't do a lot, said Uncle Griscko. They can move, they can actually walk around.
How do they walk?
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: They have tiny little suckers underneath their body and they move very slow, like much, much slower than people walk. Like over an entire day they might walk from like here to there, like a few inches. But they walk slow, but they have nothing else to do, so they just move around all the time to find food. And then when they find something to eat, like maybe a little shellfish or something, they just climb up on top of it and their mouth is underneath in the middle and they just go nom, nom, nom.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Just like eating my regular breakfast like rice cake. Or maybe they eat shrimp.
Maybe, but I think the shrimp move too fast and so they would be easily able to escape from a starfish. And Uncle Griscko said, do you have any other questions? I love your questions.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Do cats have really soft fur?
Well, I think that they do, do you think so?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Solar has rough fur and soft fur. Silver has rough fur and soft fur.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I didn't know they have rough fur. What part of them has rough fur?
What part of them... And another part, Solar doesn't have any rough parts, Silver doesn't have any rough parts, they only have soft fur.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I see.
The only green, white, and black, but not red.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: That's true. But their tongues are pink and their noses are pink.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Their noses are pink. I don't have any more questions.
Okay. Shall we finish our space mission?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so Sidseeker and Victor and Uncle Griscko flew their ship closer and closer to the sun. Now, from the Earth, the sun is too bright to look at directly, so we don't stare at it. But they had very good protection on the ship so that they could see it and look at its details as they got closer. And from the Earth it looks very small, but as they got closer it looked really big and they could see that the sun is a huge ball of fire. It's bright yellow and it's burning all the time.
And why does it have fire?
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Well, that's an interesting question. It is burning because a long time ago there was a lot of dust and rocks and gas swirling around and they collected together little by little because of gravity pulling them together and they formed a big clump and because they were pushing against each other they got really hot and then they just started to burn and it kept burning because there was so much fuel, which is the stuff that burns. And stars burn for a very long time and they make so much light and heat. And so as they got closer to the sun that's a huge ball of fire they could see all the things swirling around on the surface of the sun. There are things called sunspots which are little dark areas where it's not as hot. And there are things called flares which is where the fire leaps away from the sun and then falls back down. And sometimes it makes a huge arch up and then down, almost like a rainbow but made of fire. And there's a thing called the corona which is all around the edges of the sun where the hot gas glows. And it's very beautiful. And Victor said, wow, the sun is amazing! And Sidseeker said, I didn't know there was so much going on on the sun. I thought it was just a ball of fire but it's full of activity. And Uncle Griscko said, yes, scientists back on Earth are still studying the sun to try to understand what it's like. And one important reason we do that is because the sun is a star, just like all the other stars that are much further away. And so they look very small from the Earth, so we can't study them as easily. But the sun we can get a little closer to and learn about. And Sidseeker said, all those stars far, far away are just like the sun? And Uncle Griscko said, yes, they are. They must be so beautiful but they're too far away for us to get to. But they're also balls of fire far away.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Yeah, but you can get to them very easily if you make a U-turn. But then going back up.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: That's an interesting idea, said Uncle Griscko.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: That's a way that we can get to the very far planets, like Mars.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Well, Mars orbits around the sun also, so that's much closer than any of the stars that are so far away.
Yeah, Mars is very close. So next time we go on a space mission, we have to stay buckled in, but Victor won't leave in.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Yeah. Well, Victor can come too as long as he stays buckled up and safe.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Victor, next time we go to see a planet, Sidseeker won't lift you up. You can wait to see it when we get out of the rocket.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay, I guess I can wait, said Victor. And Sidseeker said, well, we could just adjust his seat so it's a little higher so he can see out. And Victor said, oh, that's good, thank you. And so Uncle Griscko said, well, we're using up all our fuel flying, so we better make sure we have enough to get ourselves home. We better turn around the ship. And Victor said, oh, like make a U-turn? And Uncle Griscko said, yeah, a U-turn is when you just go back the way you were coming. So we just got to turn right around. And so Uncle Griscko turned the ship around and they flew all the way back down to Earth. They landed in Uncle Griscko's backyard and they got out.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And why did the Yoda stop?
Hmm, is it a card? Oh, maybe take it out and put it back in. Okay, now that we're done with our story, I need to check on the thunder. Okay, well that's the end of stars and stories.
transcript.speaker: Dad

characters.name: Seeker
characters.description: A brave dwarven explorer and mediator, he resides in a glowing castle hub—a multiversal dock for skyships anchored within the skyline of New York City. From this urban sanctum, he conducts silver slime experiments and tends to wandering cats amidst a massive library. Frequently swapping between a red coat and a mystical purple cloak, his braided red beard remains his most eccentric tool, capable of catching small fish. A master of fusion, he combines ancient stone-craft with high-velocity mechanics, utilizing a legendary magic ladder to scale the shifting architecture of the realms. Whether brewing portal potions, harvesting floof-berries in Limbo with gemstone goggles, or aiding Buddy the Mouse, this walnut-loving student of the stars remains the hero who orchestrated the daring rescue of the world.

characters.name: Uncle Grisco
characters.description: A nomadic mole scientist and science teacher, this subterranean inventor resides in a home brimming with wondrous experiments, serving as a devoted confidant to the gopher folk. Within his laboratory of glowing elixirs and prismatic shadows, he fuses volcanic lore with the study of light to translate the planet's restless movements and the scarred history of cosmic craters. A wandering sage who maps both deep tectonic veins and celestial paths, he frequently emerges from his sprawling tunnels to share ancient botanical secrets and astronomical insights with his friend Seeker.

characters.name: Alexander
characters.description: A poised driver and student at Ginger Catasorry, this feline friend to Dimetar and Scratch Cat manages a digital Tamagotchi empire from the confines of a lunchbox. After recently representing Silver during the playdate, their steady paw was led far from the morning commute into the treacherous depths of the dark forest, where they navigated the shadows alongside Jane.

characters.name: Victor
characters.description: Clad in blue shorts and a purple shirt, Lily’s brother is a tiny baby mouse and spirited cosmic stowaway who once served as a royal gardener within the Magic Forest’s glitter-shimmering houses. Now a rugged driver of a tow truck in the big car race, he balances his mechanical duties with a quest for a celestial watering can and the composition of percussive ‘bamp-bam-bum’ beats for space-faring worms. Though he treasures the sparkly art of Uncle Grisco and Haddy, his high-speed adventures often result in muddy messes that necessitate restorative baths, leaving him in singular pursuit of the perfect hat to complete his interstellar ensemble.

characters.name: Slickdorito
characters.description: A character who assists Victor with his seatbelt for safety.

characters.name: Gravity Goldfish
characters.description: Once a soaring engine of atmospheric ruin that leveled metropolises, this sky-bound terror was transformed by a single Valentine into a silent, mended guardian. Now celebrated in song as a legendary "pretend fish," the reformed creature drifts peacefully through the clouds, keeping a gentle, protective watch over the world and its companion, Mini Mango.

characters.name: Mini Mango
characters.description: A companion of the Gravity Goldfish.

places.name: Backyard
places.description: Behind Hattie’s workshop and Uncle Griscko’s house, an eclectic sprawl of junk litters a landscape once home to a lush kelp forest, long since devoured by a mysterious shadow. A towering spaceship now looms over the site’s inaugural breach—a raw, jagged excavation where the earth was first struck and the bitten pool noodle was originally unearthed.

places.name: The Sun
places.description: A massive, fiery star at the solar system's heart, a realm of incinerating heat that serves as the final, scorching sanctuary where the lost mookball is eventually found.

places.name: The Moon
places.description: Visible from Earth as a pockmarked sphere of gray, white, and black, this low-gravity waypoint for sun-bound crews is a playground of ancient space rocks and leaping Floofers; its silver dust now serves as a silent sanctuary for a misplaced book and the long-lost mother cat.

places.name: Isabella's Lunchbox
places.description: A pretend location where Alexander manages his Tamagotchi store.

places.name: Earth
places.description: The ancestral cradle of humanity and the definitive anchor for the galactic mission, this rocky world pulses with a hidden molten core. As the home planet where the journey begins and ends, it serves as the foundational site for the Floofers’ true home and new school, bridging an ancient legacy with the education of a new generation.

places.name: Space
places.description: The weightless, black void beyond Earth’s atmosphere is a vast expanse of drifting giant rocks that serves as Captain Izzy’s silent laboratory. Now the final destination of the robot's mission, this lethal frontier requires a specialized suit to navigate its airless and crushing stillness.
